current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: Run the routed architecture in proxy at K=128 using the current strongest training settings to test a fundamentally different support-selection mechanism.
Run a proxy stabilization pass on the best gated K=128 EF=16 configuration with AUXK_ALPHA reduced from 0.03125 to 0.01.
Run a proxy stabilization pass on the best gated K=128 EF=16 configuration with AUXK_ALPHA reduced from 0.03125 to 0.01. full

recent_round_summaries.hypothesis: Severe stagnation and two non-competitive K=128 architecture probes suggest the current global-ranking support mechanisms are locally exhausted. A new `group_topk` prototype that enforces one local winner per small latent group before the global top-k may reduce redundant feature selection and improve reconstruction quality or cost under the same validated training regime.

After nine non-improving rounds and non-competitive K=128 results from topk and routed, the current global support-ranking direction looks structurally weak. The newly added `group_topk` family is the next fundamentally different architecture probe because it imposes local winner-take-all competition before global selection, which may improve feature diversity and reduce redundant support allocation under the same validated training regime.

In crash recovery mode, the safest informative move is to return to the best known working gated K=128 configuration and make the smallest remaining stabilization adjustment. A milder auxiliary pressure of AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 may preserve the strong gated baseline while avoiding the degradation seen at 0.0625, giving a cleaner read on whether the current anchor is slightly over-regularized.

topk.design_hypothesis: Five non-improving rounds on gated-specific tweaks suggest the current search direction is locally exhausted, so the next meaningful move is to revisit a fundamentally different sparse selection mechanism. A clean top-k mainline comparison at K=128 using the current strongest training regime may show whether gated-specific routing is the bottleneck rather than the optimizer or regularization settings.
next_steps: If top-k proxy FVU improves materially over its earlier result and is competitive with gated, promote it to full and begin top-k stabilization from this stronger anchor.
If top-k remains clearly worse even under the stronger regime, conclude that a different architectural direction is needed and retire top-k from near-term focus.
If top-k is competitive on quality but notably better on runtime or stability, keep it alive as an alternate mainline family for subsequent cost-quality tradeoff work.

group_topk.design_hypothesis: After nine non-improving rounds and non-competitive K=128 results from topk and routed, the current global support-ranking direction looks structurally weak. The newly added `group_topk` family is the next fundamentally different architecture probe because it imposes local winner-take-all competition before global selection, which may improve feature diversity and reduce redundant support allocation under the same validated training regime.
next_steps: If `group_topk` proxy FVU is competitive with or better than topk and routed, keep the family alive and stabilize `group_topk_size` as the next family-specific axis.
If `group_topk` is only modest on quality but shows better runtime or memory behavior, evaluate it as a Pareto-tradeoff family rather than discarding it immediately.
If `group_topk` is clearly worse on both quality and cost signals, retire this local-competition idea and move to a more expressive multi-branch or factorized encoder family.

recent_performance_findings: round 3: suspected performance regression for jumprelu (tps=16601.650675, baseline_ratio=0.200000, termination=throughput_too_low)
next_move_guidance: If AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 improves proxy FVU or training stability, promote it to full as the new gated stabilization candidate.
If AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 is also worse, conclude that the current gated anchor is not improved by AUXK retuning and keep the original 0.03125 setting as the stable baseline.

If this run shows instability despite using the best known working setup, continue crash recovery with another minimal gated parameter adjustment rather than returning to architecture exploration immediately.
